qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe pickle deserialization in Delta constructor permits class pollution and RCE
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes class pollution via dunder attribute traversal (__globals__/__builtins__) during delta application setattr (delta.py:306, path.py:138-140), which is a different root cause than the advisory's memory exhaustion through unconstrained constructor arguments of SAFE_TO_IMPORT types (serialization.py:353, delta.py:576). The advisory explicitly notes the class pollution attack was already addressed by CVE-2025-58367. The finding tests v8.6.0 vs v8.6.1 (dunder traversal patch), while the advisory's affected range extends to 8.6.2 (constructor argument fix).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes memory exhaustion DoS through unconstrained constructor arguments of SAFE_TO_IMPORT types (bytes(N), list(N), range(N)) in _RestrictedUnpickler. The only qualifying finding describes a different vulnerability: class pollution and RCE via dunder attribute traversal during delta application setattr. These have distinct root causes, distinct vulnerable code paths (serialization.py:353/delta.py:576 vs delta.py:306/path.py:138-140), different CWE classifications (CWE-400/770 vs deserialization), and different version ranges (fixed in 8.6.2 vs 8.6.1).
evidence: Advisory root cause: _RestrictedUnpickler.find_class does not limit constructor arguments for SAFE_TO_IMPORT types like builtins.bytes
Advisory vulnerable paths: serialization.py:353 (find_class check), delta.py:576 (new_type without size guard)
Finding root cause: uncontrolled setattr during delta application allows dunder attribute traversal
Finding vulnerable paths: delta.py:306 (setattr), path.py:138-140 (dunder traversal check)
Advisory states CVE-2025-58367 already hardened against class pollution attacks, indicating the finding describes a prior vulnerability
Advisory fixed in 8.6.2; finding's described patch is in 8.6.1
